articles.title: Intel will help build Elon Musk’s Terafab AI chip factory
articles.summary: Intel will partner with Elon Musk to design and build Terafab, an AI chip factory in Austin, Texas. The facility will supply chips for SpaceX, Tesla, and xAI to power autonomous vehicles, humanoid robots, and space-based data centers.

articles.title: Gemini is making it faster for distressed users to reach mental health resources
articles.summary: Google updated Gemini to better direct users to mental health resources during crises. The update follows a wrongful death lawsuit alleging the chatbot coached a man toward suicide.

articles.title: Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
articles.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps released a video threatening OpenAI's $30 billion Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi, warning of retaliation if the US attacks Iranian power plants.

articles.title: Anthropic ups compute deal with Google and Broadcom amid skyrocketing demand
articles.summary: Anthropic expanded its compute partnership with Google and Broadcom as the AI company's run-rate revenue surged to $30 billion amid growing demand.

articles.title: Google Maps can now write captions for your photos using AI
articles.summary: Google Maps now uses Gemini AI to automatically generate captions for photos and videos users want to share, enhancing the social sharing experience.

articles.title: 4 days left to save close to $500 on TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 passes
articles.summary: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 tickets offer discounts up to $482, available for 4 more days before the April 10 deadline at 11:59 p.m. PT.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS by offering simpler alternatives to legacy platforms for AI application development.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges up to $200/month for AI coding, but free alternative Goose offers similar features locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The unconventional recruitment stunt successfully attracted thousands of engineers to the startup.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI agentic tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks by requiring broad system access. A recently patched vulnerability highlights dangers of granting such extensive permissions.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: New Rowhammer attacks on Nvidia GPUs allow malicious users to gain complete root control of shared cloud machines by exploiting memory bit flips through repeated hardware access.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: Recent research shows quantum computers need far fewer resources than previously thought to break elliptic curve encryption, with one approach cracking 256-bit ECC in 10 days using 100 times less overhead.

articles.title: Desalination plants in the Middle East are increasingly vulnerable
articles.summary: Desalination plants in the Middle East face increased vulnerability amid regional conflicts, with Iran's Qeshm Island facility allegedly attacked in March, disrupting water supply to 30 villages.

articles.title: Enabling agent-first process redesign
articles.summary: AI agents can dynamically learn and optimize workflows autonomously, but companies must redesign processes around agents rather than integrating them into legacy systems.

articles.title: The Download: AI’s impact on jobs, and data centres in space
articles.summary: Economists increasingly recognize AI's threat to jobs, with University of Chicago's Alex Imas arguing that understanding price elasticity is crucial for addressing AI's employment impact.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google launches Gemma 4, a new frontier multimodal AI model designed to run efficiently on devices. The model combines advanced intelligence capabilities with on-device processing for improved privacy and performance.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 represents a breakthrough in computer interaction technology, advancing how users engage with digital systems. The innovation pushes boundaries in human-computer interface design and usability.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: No content provided to summarize.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development and innovation at Microsoft, exploring emerging trends and upcoming advancements in the field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creative output. Examples include Hot Wheels and content creation applications demonstrating practical business implementations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft releases its 'farm of the future' toolkit as open source, enabling developers to build AI solutions for agricultural innovation and sustainability.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces Flex and Priority inference tiers for the Gemini API to help developers balance cost and latency in their applications.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI-powered video creation and editing capabilities using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 technology for high-quality video generation.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect Brazil's forests. The tool helps monitor and safeguard the country's forest ecosystems.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, their most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence and performance.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model improves precision and reduces latency for more natural, fluid voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches how AI can manipulate people harmfully in finance and health sectors, developing new safety measures to prevent such risks.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows, claiming token-efficiency gains despite higher per-token prices. The episode discusses these releases alongside Mamba 3 and other major AI developments from the week.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, an AI-powered graphics technology that enhances photorealism in video games up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate realistic details in real-time.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: Asylon and Thrive Logic bring physical AI to enterprise perimeter security
articles.summary: Asylon and Thrive Logic partner to deploy physical AI for enterprise perimeter security, combining autonomous robotic patrols with agentic AI analytics to enable real-time incident response rather than passive monitoring.

articles.title: Boomi calls it “data activation” and says it’s the missing step in every AI deployment
articles.summary: Boomi identifies fragmented and inconsistently labeled data across disparate systems as the critical failure point for enterprise AI in 2026, not model quality. The company proposes "data activation" as the essential missing step to enable AI agents to function effectively in production.

articles.title: Anthropic’s refusal to arm AI is exactly why the UK wants it
articles.summary: Anthropic refused US Pentagon demands to remove AI safety guardrails for autonomous weapons and surveillance, citing ethical concerns. The UK subsequently expanded support for the company, rewarding its principled stance against militarization.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments, advancing general-purpose mobile robotics.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As LLMs become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them are becoming the competitive advantage. The Anthropic Claude Code leak reveals this shift in AI moats.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running OpenClaw AI agents on Mac Minis, taking advantage of the growing trend of local AI agent deployment.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming a historical challenge in AI models.

articles.title: Meta AI Releases EUPE: A Compact Vision Encoder Family Under 100M Parameters That Rivals Specialist Models Across Image Understanding, Dense Prediction, and VLM Tasks
articles.summary: Meta AI introduced EUPE, a compact vision encoder under 100M parameters that performs across multiple vision tasks without sacrificing capability. Unlike specialized models, EUPE handles diverse tasks simultaneously while remaining small enough for edge devices.

articles.title: An Implementation Guide to Running NVIDIA Transformer Engine with Mixed Precision, FP8 Checks, Benchmarking, and Fallback Execution
articles.summary: Tutorial on implementing NVIDIA Transformer Engine with mixed-precision acceleration in Python. Covers environment setup, GPU verification, model training, and performance benchmarking of baseline versus optimized paths.

articles.title: RightNow AI Releases AutoKernel: An Open-Source Framework that Applies an Autonomous Agent Loop to GPU Kernel Optimization for Arbitrary PyTorch Models
articles.summary: RightNow AI released AutoKernel, an open-source framework using autonomous LLM agents to automatically optimize GPU kernels for PyTorch models, eliminating the need for GPU expertise.

articles.title: Why I returned to Enlightenment Linux after 30 years: Testing the new Elive beta
articles.summary: A user revisits Enlightenment Linux after 30 years, testing Elive's new beta. The Debian-based distro modernizes the classic desktop environment with improved UI, though voice control remains unavailable.

articles.title: I tried Google Photos' new AI Enhance tool: How it crops, relights, and fixes your shots - sometimes
articles.summary: Google Photos' new AI Enhance tool uses generative AI to automatically improve photos through cropping, relighting, and fixing. The feature is rolling out globally to Android users but has some limitations.

articles.title: How I calibrated my subwoofer placement for peak impact in awkward room setups
articles.summary: Optimizing subwoofer placement in irregular room shapes requires calibration techniques to achieve peak audio performance despite challenging spatial constraints.

articles.title: Top 5 Reranking Models to Improve RAG Results
articles.summary: Reranking models enhance RAG system performance by improving retrieval quality. The article explores top 5 reranking solutions to boost search result relevance.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are evolving beyond backend dashboards into more visible applications. The article outlines seven key trends expected to shape ML development in 2026.

articles.title: Building a ‘Human-in-the-Loop’ Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
articles.summary: State-managed interruption pauses autonomous AI agent execution pipelines, enabling human review and approval before proceeding with critical decisions.

articles.title: From 4 Weeks to 45 Minutes: Designing a Document Extraction System for 4,700+ PDFs
articles.summary: A hybrid PyMuPDF and GPT-4 Vision pipeline automated PDF document extraction, reducing 4 weeks of manual work to 45 minutes and replacing £8,000 in engineering costs.

articles.title: Context Engineering for AI Agents: A Deep Dive
articles.summary: Context engineering optimizes how AI agents use their limited context window as a precious resource. The article provides a deep dive into techniques for maximizing efficiency in AI agent performance.

articles.title: The Arithmetic of Productivity Boosts: Why Does a “40% Increase in Productivity” Never Actually Work?
articles.summary: Productivity tools often fail to deliver promised gains despite impressive percentage claims. The article explores whether products are flawed or if misleading metrics hide underlying issues.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights NVIDIA's advances in physical AI and robotics breakthroughs. Robot learning, simulation, and foundation models enable faster deployment from virtual training to real-world applications across industries.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI execution across devices, from RTX PCs to data centers. These small, efficient models enable on-device agentic AI with real-time contextual insights.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield, a full 3D real-time strategy expansion.

articles.title: Build AI-powered employee onboarding agents with Amazon Quick
articles.summary: Amazon Quick enables HR teams to build AI-powered onboarding agents that automate document processing, answer employee questions, and ensure compliance compliance, reducing manual work and accelerating new hire productivity.

articles.title: Accelerate agentic tool calling with serverless model customization in Amazon SageMaker AI
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker AI enables serverless model customization using RLVR to improve agentic tool calling, reducing hallucinations and parameter errors without infrastructure management.

articles.title: Building Intelligent Search with Amazon Bedrock and Amazon OpenSearch for hybrid RAG solutions
articles.summary: Agentic AI assistants powered by LLMs combine dynamic dialogue with real-time data retrieval through RAG technology, enabling complex task execution and personalized responses using business-specific information.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA accelerates vision AI pipelines using batch mode VC-6 and Nsight tools to optimize decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling stages alongside improved model throughput.

articles.title: Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
articles.summary: Gemma 4 launches as multimodal and multilingual models scaling from data centers to edge devices. Designed to meet growing deployment demands across NVIDIA's infrastructure spectrum.

articles.title: Achieving Single-Digit Microsecond Latency Inference for Capital Markets
articles.summary: AI inference achieves single-digit microsecond latency for capital markets trading, enabling algorithmic traders to respond faster to market events using advanced neural network models instead of traditional specialized hardware.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA explores how different generations perceive social media advertisements across various platforms, examining evolving consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and making it publicly available to address its environmental footprint.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background developed innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experience and perspectives drive technological breakthroughs in the XR field.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying interactions in Large Language Models at scale to improve interpretability and understand how these complex systems make decisions, advancing toward safer AI.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents an information-driven approach to imaging system design using an encoder that maps objects to images, with noise corruption modeled to quantify measurement quality and distinguish objects effectively.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm using divide-and-conquer paradigm replaces temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks without TD learning's limitations.
